_comment: Caveau folder marker. Copy this as '.caveau-guard.json' INTO a client folder to protect that folder (and everything under it). This is the Cowork-native way — Cowork can write this file into a folder you've connected, unlike ~/.config. The guard walks up from each accessed file to find the nearest marker. An empty {} also works (just protects the folder).
_allow_paths_doc: Sub-paths inside this folder that stay readable despite the protection — e.g. the 'clean/' folder holding anonymised output. Relative to this marker's folder, or absolute.
_allow_extensions_doc: File extensions exempt inside this folder (already-cloaked output).
_block_bash_doc: Also deny shell commands that mention this folder's path (stops `cat <file>` bypassing the read guard).
_tripwire_enabled_doc: Keep the chat-box PII tripwire on (it reads the global/plugin config; this flag is informational at the folder level).
